findings.evidence: `rg` shows only private general lemmas in this file: line 56 `private theorem map_reindex_pathLaw ... (φ : ℕ → ℕ)` and line 67 `private theorem map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw ... (φ : ℕ → ℕ)`, while the exported lemmas are line 82 `map_shift_iterate_pathLaw` and line 92 `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw`.
findings.fix: Make public renamed general lemmas, e.g. `map_reindex_pathLaw` and `map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw` for any `φ : ℕ → ℕ`, then derive the shift-iterate identities from them as convenience corollaries.
findings.issue: The natural path-law API is arbitrary coordinate reindexing, but `map_reindex_pathLaw` and `map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw` are private while the public lemmas at lines 82 and 92 expose only the special case `φ n = n + r`.

summary: The path-law shift identities are exported only as additive-shift special cases even though the file proves the arbitrary reindexing statements internally. That leaves the public API below the natural Mathlib/TauCeti level and invites duplicated downstream lemmas.
